factors.summary: The company's balance sheet is strengthening through deleveraging and refinancing at lower rates.

evidence.text: In April 2020, the Company entered into a 364-day, supplemental $400 million revolving credit facility (the “2020 Revolving Credit Agreement”). This supplemental facility was never utilized by the Company prior to its expiration in April 2021.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining buybacks and dividends, signaling confidence.

evidence.text: In the first six months of 2021, we repurchased 1.6 million shares of our outstanding common stock under the Company’s share repurchase program for $156.0 million. [...] On July 23, 2021, our Board of Directors authorized the repurchase of up to $400 million of shares of our common stock over the two years ending July 23, 2023.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $262.7 million in the six months ended June 30, 2021, compared to net cash provided by operating activities of $260.5 million in the six months ended June 30, 2020. [...] The increase in cash provided of $2.2 million was primarily due to higher net income, partially offset by higher increases in working capital associated with our sales growth.

factors.summary: The company is successfully integrating acquisitions, creating value.

evidence.text: In January 2021, we obtained control of and began consolidating Flo in our Plumbing segment results. [...] The fair value allocated to assets acquired and liabilities assumed as of January 1, 2021 was $87.8 million, net of cash acquired of $9.7 million.

factors.summary: The company faces no material litigation exposure.

evidence.text: We are defendants in lawsuits associated with the normal conduct of our businesses and operations. [...] The Company believes the likelihood of material loss is remote.

factors.summary: Environmental compliance is manageable and not creating significant costs.

evidence.text: Compliance with federal, state and local laws regulating the discharge of materials into the environment [...] did not have a material effect on capital expenditures, earnings or the competitive position of Fortune Brands [...] We believe compliance with current environmental protection laws [...] will not have a material adverse effect upon our results of operations, cash flows or financial condition.

factors.summary: Interest rate changes are not significantly impacting the company.

evidence.text: The adoption of recent accounting standards [...] has not had and is not expected to have a significant impact on our revenue, earnings or liquidity.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are creating both favorable and unfavorable impacts.

evidence.text: We have operations in various foreign countries [...] Therefore, changes in the value of the related currencies affect our financial statements when translated into U.S. dollars.

factors.summary: No significant geopolitical risks are mentioned.

evidence.text: There have been no material changes to the risk factors previously disclosed [...] in the section entitled 'Risk Factors.'
